mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.25%, the Federal Reserve maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses equity valuations and increases bond yields as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as the market prices in sustained policy restraint.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced aggregate demand. This triggers expectations of future Fed rate cuts and lower inflation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall as investors shift to safe-haven bonds and repricing occurs for lower future policy rates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.25 percentage points in 1 month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.25pp, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and yield direction is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously, it indicates weakening aggregate demand and reduced inflation pressure, prompting expectations of monetary accommodation and lower Treasury yields over the forward 20-day window.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.5% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of co-declining RSAFS/HOUST instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.5 indicates robust supply-side expansion and capacity utilization. This sustains inflation expectations and reduces the likelihood of near-term rate cuts, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as the market reprices inflation risk and policy duration.
regime_hint: supply-driven expansion with sustained capacity utilization
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO > 104.5, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth slows below 100k/month while UNRATE remains below 4.5%, it signals a transition from tight to loosening labor conditions. This reduces wage-driven inflation expectations and causes Treasury yields to fall as rate-cut probabilities increase.
regime_hint: labor market transition from tightness to loosening
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 323.5 signals persistent price pressures that force the Fed to maintain or raise the policy rate. This keeps real rates elevated and increases term premiums on long-duration assets. The 10-year Treasury yield rises as the market prices in sustained monetary restraint and inflation risk.
regime_hint: persistent inflation regime requiring policy restraint
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL > 323.5, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 1.5% annualized signals economic stagnation and reduced inflation expectations. Combined with CPIAUCSL below 323.0, this indicates a disinflationary environment where the Fed will cut rates. The 10-year Treasury yield falls as investors reprice duration and safe-haven demand increases.
regime_hint: low-growth, disinflationary regime with policy easing bias
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of instances meeting both conditions, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 103.5 combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.4%) signal supply-side weakness and labor market softening. This reduces inflation expectations and increases recession risk, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to fall sharply as investors flee to safe-haven duration and the Fed is expected to cut rates.
regime_hint: supply-side contraction with labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of instances meeting both conditions, or the interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
